nameMeanings.name: Denton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called. The vast majority, including those in Cambridgeshire, Cumbria, Dumfries, Durham, Kent, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northumberland, Oxfordshire, Sussex, and Yorkshire are named from Old English denu ‘valley’ (see Dean 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. An isolated example in Northamptonshire appears in the Domesday Book as Dodintone meaning ‘enclosure, settlement associated with Dodda or Dudda’.

variants.name: Dean
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton was born in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of John Denton and Isabell Brome. He married Mrs. Thomas Denton in 1609. He died on 10 March 1582, in Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton est né à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de John Denton et Isabell Brome. Il a épousé Mrs. Thomas Denton en 1609. Il est décédé le 10 mars 1582, à Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton wurde in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von John Denton und Isabell Brome geboren. Er hat 1609 Mrs. Thomas Denton geheiratet. Er starb am 10. März 1582 in Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton è nato il nel Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di John Denton e Isabell Brome. Lui sposato Mrs. Thomas Denton nel 1609. Morì il 10 marzo 1582, nel Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas DentonはイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアでJohn DentonとIsabell Bromeの息子として生まれた。1609年にMrs. Thomas Dentonと結婚した。彼は1582年3月10日にイギリスヨークシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 John Denton(와)과 Isabell Brome의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1609년 Mrs. Thomas Denton(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1582년 3월 10일 영국 요르크실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton nasceu em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de John Denton e Isabell Brome. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Thomas Denton em 1609. Ele faleceu em 10 de março de 1582, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton родился в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали John Denton и Isabell Brome. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Thomas Denton в 1609 г. Он умер 10 марта 1582 г. в местности еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas Denton nació en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de John Denton y Isabell Brome. Se casó con Mrs. Thomas Denton en 1609. Murió el 10 de marzo de 1582, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
